title: Jasonia antiatlantica
intro: Jasonia antiatlantica, also known as Atlantic jasonia or Moroccan jasonia, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Morocco. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Jasonia antiatlantica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Jasonia antiatlantica is prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in some commercial herbal supplements, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Jasonia antiatlantica is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Canary Islands and is also known as the Canary Islands jasonia. This plant is a part of the genus Jasonia, which is a group of plants that are commonly found in the western Mediterranean region. The exact classification of Jasonia antiatlantica may vary depending on the source, but it is widely recognized as a distinct species within the Asteraceae family.
